statement.p: f(x) = |3-2x| is not invertible. Break the domain of $f$ into two components on which each component is invertible. Find the inverse function for each invertible segment.
On the restricted domain [1.5,\infty)\text{,} f(x) is equal to the function g(x) = , and has an inverse function of g^{-1} = .
On the restricted domain (-\infty, 1.5)\text{,} f(x) is equal to the function h(x) = , and has an inverse function of h^{-1} = .

solution.p: In general, for a function f(x)\text{,} |f(x)| = \pm f(x)\text{.} So, our two functions are 3-2x and -(3-2x) = 2x-3\text{.} These are on the domains (-\infty,1.5) and [1.5,\infty)\text{,} respectively.
